methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $318M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Svante, a Series E climate and energy technology company founded by Claude Letourneau, has raised $318M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of nine comparable companies—one public (LNZA) and eight private firms including TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and QuantumScape—though meaningful cross-stage comparables within the Climate & Energy sector remain limited.

A stage-progression methodology, anchored to the company's $318M total raised as a valuation proxy, applies a 1.4x multiplier typical of Series E advancement to successor rounds. This yields an implied valuation of $445M. A peer-median approach was not applicable; while 21 same-sector peers exist, insufficient clustering at identical stage, adjacent stage, and comparable tier prevented reliable median calculation without risking selection bias.

The triangulated valuation range is $378M (low) to $512M (high), with a median point of $445M. This single-method result carries material uncertainty. Key dependencies include the appropriateness of the 1.4x step-up multiplier for Svante's specific profile and market conditions, and the representativeness of the broader peer set given sparse direct comparables at Series E within climate tech. Revenue trajectory, technology maturity, and path to commercialization would be critical refinements to this framework.
